title: Connect the Uniface Router Monitor to the Uniface Router
breadcrumbs: Connect the Uniface Router Monitor to the Uniface Router
text: Connect the Uniface Router Monitor to the Uniface Router You can connect a single Router Monitor to only one Uniface Router at a time. However, it is possible to have multiple Monitors connected to a single Uniface Router. Use the $MAXMONS assignment setting in urouter.asn to specify the number of Router Monitors allowed to connect to the Uniface Router. To connect the Uniface Router Monitor to a Uniface Router: Start the Uniface Router Monitor. By default, the Connect dialog is displayed, but this behavior can be altered by editing the monitor settings. If the Router Monitor is running but not connected, choose URouter > Connect If you are already connected and want to connect to another Uniface Router, choose URouter > Disconnect , then choose URouter > Connect . In the Connect dialog, provide the following information: Table 1. Uniface Router Monitor Connection Fields Field Description Hostname Identifier of the host on which the Uniface Router is running; for example, localhost or LABHOST-PC0001 , or an IP address. Network protocol Network protocol used to connect the Router Monitor to the Uniface Router: TCP or TLS . Address / port Address or port on the host that the Uniface Router is listening to. By default, this is 13001. Note: You can append a TLS profile to the port number to specify an explicit (non-default) profile. For more information, see TLS Connection Profiles . Become privileged user If selected, the following fields are displayed: Username —user name must be the same as the account under which the Uniface Router is running, or ‘ UROUTER ’. If TCP is used, it is not hidden, scrambled, or encoded. If TLS is used, the information is protected. Password —It is not scrambled or encoded, but is replaced by ‘ *** ’ in the forms where it appears. See also $PRIV_MON_USER . Note: The user name and password will be authenticated on the machine running the Uniface Router, so they must represent a valid account on that machine. To specify the default values for these fields, see Specify Preferences for the Uniface Router Monitor . If the Uniface Router is not running, or if it is listening to a different port number, the Router Monitor cannot make a connection. Related Links Uniface Router Monitor The Uniface Router Monitor is a Uniface Windows application that you can use to fine-tune the performance of the Uniface Router while it is running. Uniface Router The Uniface Router is a multi-threaded process (urouter) responsible for interprocess communication in Uniface applications. It listens for requests from Uniface client applications and routes them to the appropriate Uniface Server, and it passes messages between various Uniface applications. $MAX_MONS Specify the maximum number of Uniface Router Monitors that the Uniface Router can accept.
